questions.question: Prove that work done by a variable force along a straight line is given by the area under the force-displacement graph.
questions.answer: For a variable force F(x), infinitesimal work dW = F(x) dx. Total work, W = ∫ F(x) dx over the displacement, which equals the area under the F–x graph.

questions.question: A body of mass 5 kg slides down a frictionless incline of height 10 m. Calculate its speed at the bottom using energy conservation.
questions.answer: PE at top = mgh = 5 × 9.8 × 10 = 490 J. KE at bottom = 1/2 m v² = 490 ⇒ v² = 196 ⇒ v ≈ 14 m/s.

questions.question: Explain the work-energy theorem and derive its mathematical expression.
questions.answer: Work done by net force on a body = Change in kinetic energy. W = F × d = ΔKE = 1/2 m v² - 1/2 m u².

questions.question: A 2 kg object is acted upon by a force F(x) = 10x N over a displacement from x = 0 to x = 5 m. Calculate the work done.
Derive the expression for the total mechanical energy of a body in free fall.
questions.answer: Total mechanical energy, E = KE + PE = 1/2 m v² + m g h. Using v² = 2 g (h₀ - h), E = constant = m g h₀.

questions.question: A block is pushed along a horizontal surface with friction. If 500 J of work is done and 100 J is lost to friction, find the increase in kinetic energy.
questions.answer: Work done = ΔKE + Work done against friction ⇒ ΔKE = 500 - 100 = 400 J.

questions.question: Show that the work done by gravity on a body moving along a vertical path depends only on the vertical displacement.
questions.answer: W = F × h = m g × vertical height. Independent of the path taken, depends only on initial and final vertical positions.

questions.question: A body of mass m slides down a frictionless incline of height h. Derive its velocity at the bottom using energy conservation and kinematics.
questions.answer: Using conservation of energy: m g h = 1/2 m v² ⇒ v = √(2 g h). Using v² = u² + 2 a s (a = g sinθ, s = h / sinθ), same result is obtained.

questions.question: Explain the difference between conservative and non-conservative forces with examples.
questions.answer: Conservative forces (gravity, spring) depend only on initial and final positions; W = -ΔPE. Non-conservative forces (friction, air resistance) depend on path; W is lost as heat.

questions.question: A body of mass 3 kg is dropped from 20 m. Calculate work done by gravity and kinetic energy just before hitting the ground.
questions.answer: Work done by gravity = m g h = 3 × 9.8 × 20 = 588 J. KE at bottom = 588 J.

questions.question: A car accelerates from 10 m/s to 20 m/s with a mass of 1000 kg. Calculate the work done by the engine.
Derive the expression for kinetic energy using work done by a constant force.
questions.answer: W = F × d = m a × d; using v² = u² + 2 a d, W = m (v² - u²)/2 = ΔKE = 1/2 m v² - 1/2 m u².

questions.question: A 50 kg mass is lifted vertically by 4 m. If the lifting is done in 5 s, calculate the power developed.
questions.answer: Work done = m g h = 50 × 9.8 × 4 = 1960 J. Power = W / t = 1960 / 5 ≈ 392 W.

questions.question: A body of mass 2 kg moves under a variable force F(x) = 5 + x N from x = 0 to x = 4 m. Calculate the work done.
A pendulum of length 2 m is released from 30° angle. Find the speed at the lowest point using energy conservation.
Explain why work done by friction is negative and derive an expression for it.
questions.answer: Friction opposes motion ⇒ displacement opposite to force ⇒ work done W = - f × d.

questions.question: A block slides down an inclined plane of height h and length l with friction. Derive an expression for work done against friction.
questions.answer: W_friction = f × l, where f = μ m g cosθ. Total work done by gravity = m g h; kinetic energy = m g h - μ m g cosθ × l.

questions.question: A body is projected vertically upwards with velocity u. Derive the maximum height in terms of u and g using energy conservation.
questions.answer: Initial KE = 1/2 m u², At max height KE = 0, PE = m g h_max ⇒ 1/2 m u² = m g h_max ⇒ h_max = u² / (2 g).

questions.question: Derive an expression for velocity of a body moving along a horizontal frictionless surface under a constant force.
questions.answer: W = F × d = ΔKE = 1/2 m v² ⇒ v = √(2 F d / m).

questions.question: A spring with spring constant k is compressed by x. Derive an expression for the work done in compressing it.
questions.answer: dW = F dx = k x dx ⇒ W = ∫0^x k x dx = 1/2 k x².

questions.question: A roller coaster car of mass 500 kg descends from a height of 25 m. Calculate its speed at the bottom ignoring friction.
Explain the relation between work done and change in potential energy for conservative forces.
questions.answer: For conservative force F, W = - ΔPE; work done by force decreases potential energy by equal amount.

questions.question: A body of mass 2 kg is moving with 4 m/s and comes to rest due to friction over 8 m. Find the force of friction.
questions.answer: Work done by friction = ΔKE = 1/2 m v² = 1/2 × 2 × 16 = 16 J. W = F × d ⇒ F = 16 / 8 = 2 N.

questions.question: Derive the expression for power in terms of force and velocity when force acts along the direction of motion.
questions.answer: P = Work / time = F × d / t = F × v (since v = d/t).

questions.question: A body slides down a smooth slope of height h and length l. Derive an expression for its acceleration along the slope using energy conservation.
questions.answer: PE lost = m g h = KE gained = 1/2 m v² ⇒ v² = 2 g h. Using v² = 2 a l ⇒ a = g h / l.
